query: You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository. The codebase is at version 0.1 (commit: a2c9b0ac). ## Issue to Resolve The recipient address is not a valid RFC-5321 address. ### Checklist - [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report. - [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible. ### App version v6.602 ### Where did you get the app from? Google Play ### Android version 13 ### Device model Moto X40 ### Steps to reproduce Edit a mail, input the recipient address end with a `/`, then send it. https://github.com/thundernest/k-9/assets/10363352/cbb7a99a-b348-4f88-adc3-3fccc1f4ba29 ### Expected behavior We can check the mail address before sending it, toast a message to tell the user that the address is invalid, or show a dialog like Gmail did below: ### Actual behavior Sending it, and showing the failure. ### Logs _No response_ ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `legacy/ui/legacy/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/view/UserInputEmailAddressParser.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.fsck.k9.view
 2→
 3→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
 4→import org.apache.james.mime4j.util.CharsetUtil
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Used to parse name & email address pairs entered by the user.
 8→ *
 9→ * TODO: Build a custom implementation that can deal with typical inputs from users who are not familiar with the
10→ *  RFC 5322 address-list syntax. See (ignored) tests in `UserInputEmailAddressParserTest`.
11→ */
12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }
25→
26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
28→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
29→}
30→
31→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)
32→

1→package com.fsck.k9.view
  2→
  3→import assertk.assertFailure
  4→import assertk.assertThat
  5→import assertk.assertions.containsExactly
  6→import assertk.assertions.isEmpty
  7→import assertk.assertions.isInstanceOf
  8→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
  9→import kotlin.test.Ignore
 10→import kotlin.test.Test
 11→
 12→class UserInputEmailAddressParserTest {
 13→    private val parser = UserInputEmailAddressParser()
 14→
 15→    @Test
 16→    fun `plain email address`() {
 17→        val addresses = parser.parse("user@domain.example")
 18→
 19→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 20→    }
 21→
 22→    @Test
 23→    fun `email address followed by space`() {
 24→        val addresses = parser.parse("user@domain.example ")
 25→
 26→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 27→    }
 28→
 29→    @Test
 30→    fun `email address in angle brackets`() {
 31→        val addresses = parser.parse("<user@domain.example>")
 32→
 33→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    @Test
 37→    fun `simple name and address`() {
 38→        val addresses = parser.parse("Name <user@domain.example>")
 39→
 40→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Name"))
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    @Test
 44→    fun `name with quoted string and address`() {
 45→        val addresses = parser.parse("\"Name\" <user@domain.example>")
 46→
 47→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Name"))
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    @Test
 51→    fun `name with multiple words and address`() {
 52→        val addresses = parser.parse("Firstname Lastname <user@domain.example>")
 53→
 54→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Firstname Lastname"))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun `name with non-ASCII characters and address`() {
 59→        val addresses = parser.parse("Käthe Gehäusegröße <user@domain.example>")
 60→
 61→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Käthe Gehäusegröße"))
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    @Test
 65→    fun `address with non-ASCII character in local part`() {
 66→        assertFailure {
 67→            parser.parse("müller@domain.example")
 68→        }.isInstanceOf<NonAsciiEmailAddressException>()
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    @Test
 72→    fun `address with non-ASCII character in domain part`() {
 73→        assertFailure {
 74→            parser.parse("user@dömain.example")
 75→        }.isInstanceOf<NonAsciiEmailAddressException>()
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    @Test
 79→    fun `multiple addresses separated by comma`() {
 80→        val addresses = parser.parse("one@domain.example,<two@domain.example>")
 81→
 82→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(
 83→            Address("one@domain.example"),
 84→            Address("two@domain.example"),
 85→        )
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    @Test
 89→    @Ignore("Currently not supported")
 90→    fun `multiple addresses separated by space`() {
 91→        val addresses = parser.parse("one@domain.example two@domain.example")
 92→
 93→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(
 94→            Address("one@domain.example"),
 95→            Address("two@domain.example"),
 96→        )
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    @Test
100→    fun `multiple addresses in angle brackets separated by space`() {
101→        val addresses = parser.parse("<one@domain.example>, <two@domain.example>")
102→
103→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(
104→            Address("one@domain.example"),
105→            Address("two@domain.example"),
106→        )
107→    }
108→
109→    @Test
110→    fun `incomplete address should not return a result`() {
111→        val addresses = parser.parse("user")
112→
113→        assertThat(addresses).isEmpty()
114→    }
115→
116→    @Test
117→    fun `incomplete address ending in @ should not return a result`() {
118→        val addresses = parser.parse("user@")
119→
120→        assertThat(addresses).isEmpty()
121→    }
122→
123→    @Test
124→    fun `name and incomplete address should not return a result`() {
125→        val addresses = parser.parse("Name <user")
126→
127→        assertThat(addresses).isEmpty()
128→    }
129→
130→    @Test
131→    @Ignore("Currently not supported")
132→    fun `name followed by address not in angle brackets`() {
133→        val addresses = parser.parse("Firstname Lastname user@domain.example")
134→
135→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Firstname LastName"))
136→    }
137→
138→    @Test
139→    @Ignore("Currently not supported")
140→    fun `name containing parenthesis`() {
141→        val addresses = parser.parse("Firstname (Nickname) Lastname <user@domain.example>")
142→
143→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Firstname (Nickname) LastName"))
144→    }
145→
146→    @Test
147→    @Ignore("Currently not supported")
148→    fun `name containing double quotes in the middle`() {
149→        val addresses = parser.parse("Firstname \"Nickname\" Lastname <user@domain.example>")
150→
151→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Firstname \"Nickname\" LastName"))
152→    }
153→}

conversation_history.content:
1→package com.fsck.k9.view
 2→
 3→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
 4→import org.apache.james.mime4j.util.CharsetUtil
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Used to parse name & email address pairs entered by the user.
 8→ *
 9→ * TODO: Build a custom implementation that can deal with typical inputs from users who are not familiar with the
10→ *  RFC 5322 address-list syntax. See (ignored) tests in `UserInputEmailAddressParserTest`.
11→ */
12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.hasInvalidHostname() -> null
21→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
22→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
23→                }
24→            }
25→    }
26→
27→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
28→
29→    private fun Address.hasInvalidHostname() = hostname?.contains("/") == true
30→
31→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
32→}
33→
34→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)
35→

1→package com.fsck.k9.view
 2→
 3→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
 4→import org.apache.james.mime4j.util.CharsetUtil
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Used to parse name & email address pairs entered by the user.
 8→ *
 9→ * TODO: Build a custom implementation that can deal with typical inputs from users who are not familiar with the
10→ *  RFC 5322 address-list syntax. See (ignored) tests in `UserInputEmailAddressParserTest`.
11→ */
12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
21→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
22→                }
23→            }
24→    }
25→
26→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
27→
28→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
29→}
30→
31→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)
32→

 12→class UserInputEmailAddressParserTest {
 13→    private val parser = UserInputEmailAddressParser()
 14→
 15→    @Test
 16→    fun `plain email address`() {
 17→        val addresses = parser.parse("user@domain.example")
 18→
 19→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 20→    }
 21→
 22→    @Test
 23→    fun `email address followed by space`() {
 24→        val addresses = parser.parse("user@domain.example ")
 25→
 26→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 27→    }
 28→
 29→    @Test
 30→    fun `email address in angle brackets`() {
 31→        val addresses = parser.parse("<user@domain.example>")
 32→
 33→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    @Test
 37→    fun `simple name and address`() {
 38→        val addresses = parser.parse("Name <user@domain.example>")
 39→
 40→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Name"))
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    @Test
 44→    fun `name with quoted string and address`() {
 45→        val addresses = parser.parse("\"Name\" <user@domain.example>")
 46→
 47→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Name"))
 48→    }
 49→
 50→    @Test
 51→    fun `name with multiple words and address`() {
 52→        val addresses = parser.parse("Firstname Lastname <user@domain.example>")
 53→
 54→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Firstname Lastname"))
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    @Test
 58→    fun `name with non-ASCII characters and address`() {
 59→        val addresses = parser.parse("Käthe Gehäusegröße <user@domain.example>")
 60→
 61→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(Address("user@domain.example", "Käthe Gehäusegröße"))
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    @Test
 65→    fun `address with non-ASCII character in local part`() {
 66→        assertFailure {
 67→            parser.parse("müller@domain.example")
 68→        }.isInstanceOf<NonAsciiEmailAddressException>()
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    @Test
 72→    fun `address with non-ASCII character in domain part`() {
 73→        assertFailure {
 74→            parser.parse("user@dömain.example")
 75→        }.isInstanceOf<NonAsciiEmailAddressException>()
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    @Test
 79→    fun `multiple addresses separated by comma`() {
 80→        val addresses = parser.parse("one@domain.example,<two@domain.example>")
 81→
 82→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(

 84→            Address("two@domain.example"),
 85→        )
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    @Test

 92→
 93→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(

 96→        )
 97→    }
 98→

101→        val addresses = parser.parse("<one@domain.example>, <two@domain.example>")
102→
103→        assertThat(addresses).containsExactly(
104→            Address("one@domain.example"),
105→            Address("two@domain.example"),
106→        )
107→    }
108→

112→

114→    }
115→

1→package com.fsck.k9.view
 2→
 3→import com.fsck.k9.mail.Address
 4→import org.apache.james.mime4j.util.CharsetUtil
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Used to parse name & email address pairs entered by the user.
 8→ *
 9→ * TODO: Build a custom implementation that can deal with typical inputs from users who are not familiar with the
10→ *  RFC 5322 address-list syntax. See (ignored) tests in `UserInputEmailAddressParserTest`.
11→ */
12→internal class UserInputEmailAddressParser {
13→
14→    @Throws(NonAsciiEmailAddressException::class)
15→    fun parse(input: String): List<Address> {
16→        return Address.parseUnencoded(input)
17→            .mapNotNull { address ->
18→                when {
19→                    address.isIncomplete() -> null
20→                    address.hasInvalidHostname() -> null
21→                    address.isNonAsciiAddress() -> throw NonAsciiEmailAddressException(address.address)
22→                    else -> Address.parse(address.toEncodedString()).firstOrNull()
23→                }
24→            }
25→    }
26→
27→    private fun Address.isIncomplete() = hostname.isNullOrBlank()
28→
29→    private fun Address.hasInvalidHostname() = hostname?.contains("/") == true
30→
31→    private fun Address.isNonAsciiAddress() = !CharsetUtil.isASCII(address)
32→}
33→
34→internal class NonAsciiEmailAddressException(message: String) : Exception(message)
35→
